summary: Migrate prototype map content away from rectangular-grid topology
acceptance_criteria: Replace or migrate the default player-facing prototype topology so district adjacency and route movement are no longer derived from a rectangular row/column grid, hex axial/cube coordinate system, sequential id order, or any hidden regular lattice.

Default prototype topology uses 12-18 low-fidelity COMPOSITE districts and 25-40 explicit route edges with variable node degree, dead ends, chokepoints, river/coast/road modes, at least one explicit isolated/no-known-route reason, one visual-neighbor pair with no route, and one visually distant pair connected by an explicit route.
The fixture must not use linear districtId-derived coordinates, uniform polygons, ring-only 1-2-3 route order, or a regular lattice as the player-facing default topology.

Use synthetic/COMPOSITE low-fidelity districts unless historical_researcher has recorded source claims; do not present exact historical borders, route importance, ports, or river claims as fact without source records.
Every district polygon has nonzero area, stable id, stable anchor, no same-layer overlap, and exactly one actual controller or explicit uncontrolled state.
Every route edge references valid endpoints, explicit mode, capacity, seasonal factors, risk/terrain class, and stable tie-break id.

Content compiler rejects missing endpoints, overlapping invalid polygons, duplicate topology ids/source ids, disconnected playable graph without explicit isolated reason, route nodes outside their district polygon, and row/column/grid/hex/lattice-only adjacency shortcuts.
required_tests: negative content-compiler tests for missing endpoint, duplicate id/source id, invalid polygon, route node outside polygon, disconnected graph, row/column/grid/hex/lattice adjacency leak
save/load round-trip if topology ids or manifest hash affect saves
notes: This task is allowed to use low-fidelity synthetic map data; it must not sneak in formal production historical map claims.
Historical/cultural risk escalates to Human Gate if contested real-world claims become unavoidable.
Because this task may touch sim-core/content-runtime authority code, systems_architect review is required in addition to historical and QA review.
next_action: Wait for simulation_engineer REVIEW handoff, then route to historical_researcher, systems_architect, and qa_reviewer.
